behaviors.description: Manifest validation rejects plugins with missing required fields
behaviors.justification: Validation is deterministic logic independent of I/O. A real failure (code execution despite invalid manifest) is caught by verifying require() is never called for invalid plugins—achievable via mocks. File system access is not needed because the validation can be tested on manifest objects directly.
behaviors.test_description: Create invalid manifest objects missing required fields (name, version, onLoad export). Mock fs and require(). Pass to the loader and verify: (1) loader rejects each with a clear validation error, (2) require() is never called for invalid plugins.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test validates the validation order without real files.

behaviors.description: Plugins are initialized in dependency order (transitive dependencies first)
behaviors.justification: Dependency resolution and topological sorting are deterministic algorithms. A real failure (wrong initialization order) is observable by spying on onLoad hook calls. Mocks are sufficient because the ordering logic does not depend on I/O or runtime variability.
behaviors.test_description: Create mock plugins with explicit dependencies forming a DAG (e.g., A→B→C). Instrument each plugin's onLoad with a spy. Load all plugins via the loader. Verify onLoad hooks are called in dependency order (C first, then B, then A).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test validates the topological sort without real files or system I/O.

behaviors.description: Circular plugin dependencies are detected and neither plugin loads
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection is a deterministic graph algorithm. Mocked plugins with circular dependencies suffice to test the detection logic. No filesystem or runtime variability is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create two mock plugins where A depends on B and B depends on A. Pass both to the loader. Verify the loader detects the cycle, reports an error, and initializes neither plugin's onLoad.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test validates cycle detection as pure algorithm logic.

behaviors.description: Plugin onLoad hook is invoked only after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed
behaviors.justification: Testing 'after module import' requires actual Node.js require() semantics and real module-level code execution. Mocking require() risks hiding failures where the loader incorrectly calls onLoad before the module's initialization code runs. Real files ensure the module's top-level code executes first.
behaviors.test_description: Create real plugin files where the module sets a global or module-scoped variable during its initialization (before exporting onLoad). Create manifest files for these plugins. Load via the loader and verify the initialization side effect occurred before onLoad is invoked.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test must verify actual module initialization timing, which requires real require() semantics and files.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws during onLoad, dependent plugins are skipped, but independent plugins continue loading
behaviors.justification: Error handling and selective initialization are testable with mocked plugins and exception injection. The real failures (loader crashes, wrong plugins skipped after an error, dependent plugins loaded despite failure) are all observable via spies on onLoad calls and by verifying which plugins initialize.
behaviors.test_description: Create mock plugins: A (no dependencies), B (no dependencies, throws in onLoad), C (depends on B), D (depends on A). Load all plugins. Verify: A initializes, B's error is logged and caught, C does not initialize (depends on failed B), D initializes (depends on A which succeeded).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test validates error handling and dependency-aware continuation logic using mocks and spies.
